author: elmoneedsarson
description: Embed and View local 3D models in your notes. Supports STL, GLB, OBJ+MTL, FBX, and 3MF file formats with full 3D scene customization

manifest.author: Jesse Strijker
manifest.description: Embed and view 3DModels in markdown notes. Supports .stl, .obj, 3mf, fbx and .gltf/.glb files.
manifest.name: Embed 3D

name: Embed 3D

releases.author: ElmoNeedsArson
releases.description: A release intended to fix the issues with the new plugin system
releases.name: Manifest id fix

releases.author: ElmoNeedsArson
releases.description: This release adressed the 500+ warnings the eslint extension for obsidian showed me. To fix the plugin acception
releases.name: 1.1.9 Eslint Warning - Code Improvement and Cleanup

releases.author: ElmoNeedsArson
releases.description:
Bugfixes:
- Changed naming convention on snapshots saved to `{timestamp}-{modelname}-3D-Embed-thumbnail.png`
- Fixed width of canvas when 3D embed is placed inside a callout
- Renamed settings tab to be more accurate
releases.name: Minor Bugfixing and Feature Request update

releases.author: ElmoNeedsArson
releases.description:
New features:
- Added a snapshot export button (camera icon) to every 3D embed overlay, alongside the existing copy and trash buttons
   - Captures the current rendered frame and saves it as a PNG to the vault
   - Filename pattern: `3D-Embed-thumbnail-{modelname}-{timestamp}.png`

New settings (Bases section):
- Snapshot folder: specify a vault folder for exported snapshots; if the folder does not exist the export is aborted with a notice
- Auto-write snapshot property: automatically writes a `3D Embed-thumbnail` wikilink property to the note's frontmatter on export — lets Bases card view use the snapshot as a cover image
- Overwrite existing snapshot: deletes the previously referenced snapshot (resolved via the `3D Embed-thumbnail` property) before saving the new one, keeping the vault tidy

Additional:
- Updated README with a new Bases section covering snapshot workflow and Base card view setup
- Fixed a pre-existing bug where the remove-codeblock button inside `createHelperButtons` used `this` instead of the passed plugin reference
releases.name: Bases Support Property + Snapshot exports

releases.author: ElmoNeedsArson
releases.description:
New features:
- Added a dedicated 3D file viewer tab. Left-clicking any supported 3D file (stl, glb, obj, fbx, 3mf) in the file explorer now opens it in its own tab, no note embedding required.
- The viewer applies all global plugin settings (background color, lighting, camera, scale, auto-rotation, etc.)

Bugfixes:
- Fixed ResizeObserver feedback loop where resizing the pane would lock the renderer size and stop future resize events from firing
- Fixed scrollbar feedback loop on resize caused by canvas overflow expanding the container
- Fixed a cascade of resize events firing rapidly during pane splits, now coalesced with a requestAnimationFrame debounce

Additional:
- Updated README with a new section distinguishing between embedding a 3D model in a note vs. opening one directly, including a  table of contents
releases.name: Direct Model Viewer

releases.author: ElmoNeedsArson
releases.description:
## New features:
- Added a global setting for a models standard position
- Added a global setting for a models standard rotation
releases.name: Added Rotation and Position to Global Settings

releases.author: ElmoNeedsArson
releases.description:
New features:
- moved lightsource delete button to collapsed bar, instead of having to open it first in the settings Tab
- Add a remove codeblock button - overlay same as edit codeblock
- Add a copy render button - overlay same as edit codeblock
- Added an "auto" mode for the rowHeight in a grid
- HDR loading! Even more realistic lighting and rendering. 

Bugfixes:
- Made error use css variables instead of hardcoding
- Fixed bug where non-scissor grid view still showed GUI
- Models were loading as different types causing issues with the GUI when saving transformations and raycasting
- fixed a bug where config colors for hemisphere light weren't getting applied to the lightsource

Dev note:
- Cleaned up a lot of the code, of course more can still be done, but the code base is becoming more manageable.
releases.name: V1.1.2 Cleanup Release + HDRI's!

releases.author: ElmoNeedsArson
releases.description:
New features:
- Single scene command now works with selections beside cursor on model line. 
   - E.g. you can now add multiple models into the scene at once with the 'Single Scene' command if they are selected. 
- FAQ section in README.md
- Improved standard loading view
   - Added shadows
   - Added showroom floor for shadows
   - Changed default settings for better standard look
- Added standard folding of cells in grid view codeblock for visual compactness and instant rendering
- Added an error message for the codeblock to show where what went wrong and common solutions

New settings:
- Added a dropdown setting to make it possible to start a scene transparent -> instead of/beside color picker - transparent by default
- Added a toggle for having a shadowplane in the scene to see ground shadows - enabled by default
- Added a toggle for directional, spot and area lights to be able to cast shadows - enabled by default

Bugfixes:
- Fixed bug that when codeblock sets color to transparent, gui switches it back to standard color after saving?
- Fixed bug that obj files without mtl files couldnt be loaded. 

Additional:
- Improved css for lighting settings in settings tab with hover animations, transistions and better color choices
releases.name: V1.1.1 Aesthetics Update

releases.author: ElmoNeedsArson
releases.description:
This update, contains a bunch of bugfixes and a new big feature. Grid Support!

New features:
- Added Grid view support, with and without scissoring.
- Added .mtl load for .obj models. 

Bugfixes:
- Fixed resize issues for the new alignment options
- Fixed reading and editing view bugs
- Fixed gap size inconsistencies in scissor grid view
- Fixed bug in scissor view where orbit controls dont work if there is just 1 row. 
- Fixed bug in the orbit controls, where they take the gaps between scenes into account in the scissor grid function, while the gaps should not control the scenes. 
- Fixed bug for the non-scissor grid mode where its too wide, the more rows or columns there are
- Fixed bug where gap values are not used in non-scissor grid mode

Added Settings:
- Grid Subsection: Standard amount of columns for grid
- Grid Subsection: Standard rowHeight for rows in grid
- Grid Subsection: Standard Horizontal Gap size in grid between scenes
- Grid Subsection: Standard Vertical Gap size in grid between scenes
- Grid Subsection: Toggle for scissor view (Enabled by default)
- General Scene Settings: Orbit damping toggle, allows the orbit controls to be damped and smoothed

As per any global settings, they have also been added to codeblock configuration (except for scissor toggle)

Added Codeblock Configs
```json
"gridSettings": {
   "columns": 3,
   "rowHeight": 200,
   "gapX": 10,
   "gapY": 10
},
```
And
```json
"scene": {
   "orbitControlDamping": true,
}
```
releases.name: 3D Embed Grid Update

releases.author: ElmoNeedsArson
releases.description:
Fixed the resize responsiveness of the render blocks by using percentage instead of px in the css. 

Fixed bug that chose first 3d block in a note to set the width of other 3d blocks in the same note
releases.name: Bugfixes for alignment update 1.0.8

releases.author: ElmoNeedsArson
releases.description: A lot has changed, the entire codeblock configuration was switched to a more future proof and expandable layout to fit more models in one scene, more cameras, lightsources, etc. Making the codeblock more compact while adding more customization for the user. This release unfortunately breaks the old releases of the plugin. The users will be informed of this upon updating by a modal.
releases.name: Update of codeblock syntax

releases.author: ElmoNeedsArson
releases.description: This is a patch for the previous release which included a couple of irrelevant logging statements
releases.name: Removal of Unnecessary Logging Statements

releases.author: ElmoNeedsArson
releases.description:
# General Improvements
This release comes with a bunch of quality of life improvements and providing more scene control to the users.

## Setting Panel Cleanup
I cleaned up the settings panel with subheadings indicating what the settings talk about.

## Added obligatory settings
Every 3D embed needs a minimum of a few settings. It will provide the user with a warning if it does not have these settings. The easiest is just to re execute the command of embedding. 

## Added/Improved config options:
For the gui overlay button, some options were added to allow users to easily manipulate the scene. Check the README for explanation of how to use it. 
```
"showGuiOverlay": false,
```
The images below show the transform and rotation controls
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1236937b-7a8a-4482-9cfd-0743a5c0d932)
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/17599408-8fc3-4656-b01c-7732db56be78)

If a user wants it they can attach a light to the camera, and subsequently change the color and intensity of this light
```
"attachLightToCam": false,
"lightColor_AttachedCam": "FFFFFF",
"lightStrength_AttachedCam":1,
```

A user can also change the current lighting in the scene, by setting the position, color, strength and whether you want to see an indicator of where the lamp currently is. 
```
"lightColor": "ffffff",
```
```
"lightStrength":1,
```
```
"showLight":false,
```
```
"lightPosXYZ": [5,10,5],
```

## "Backend" Improvements
I implemented a better management for the renderers I use from three.js. Previously every new codeblock trigger that was done caused a new renderer to be created. So even one scene could have created 20 renderers by just changing small options in the config and rendering again. This did not function very well since webGL only has 16 active context at one time, so once the plugin surpassed that number the oldest renderers were being dropped resulting in a white screen. 

This can be fixed by retriggering the codeblock manually, but is ofcourse not optimal. I have now implemented a rendermanager which disposes and repurposes renderers for all the scenes in the plugin. This limits the maximum amount of renderers used at one moment to 16. So you cant render 16 scenes at once. Once you surpass that the oldest one will be lost. Now I generally assume people wont need to render 16 models at once, but if you do, please contact me.
releases.name: Lighting Control + GUI + RenderManager

releases.author: ElmoNeedsArson
releases.description:
This release comes with a bunch of quality of life improvements and providing more scene control to the users

Altered standard camera position to look better
- bit of a top view looking down

added config options:
```
  "showGuiOverlay": false, --> if you want to show a gui overlay on your scene !-still in development-!
  "stlColorHexString": "606060", --> provides control of the color of an stl model
  "backgroundColorHexString": "7abbd6", --> renamed --> originally named "colorHexString"
  "orthographic": true, --> provides the user with a orthographic perspective
  "camPosXYZ": [0,5,10], --> allows the user to set the position of the camera in the scene
  "LookatXYZ": [0,0,0], --> provides a lookat position of the camera !-also influences orbit controls-!
  "showAxisHelper": false, "length": 5, --> shows an axis helper
  "showGridHelper": false, "gridSize": 10 --> shows a grid helper
```

Added GUI interactions
- colorpicker for background
- axis and grid helper checkbox

added to settings tab (these settings when toggled with automatically when embedding a 3D model default to a certain value):
- Toggle orthographic camera
- toggle GUI overlay
releases.name: Added much more configuration options

releases.author: ElmoNeedsArson
releases.description:
In this release support was added for 3 3D model types:
.3mf
.fbx
.obj

A setting was added to the settings tab. This setting controls, if you execute the command of adding a 3d scene, whether your object will automatically rotate

The codeblock now contains the option to show axisHelpers and gridHelpers. These can be defined with a custom size
releases.name: Added Support for 3 More file types | added setting

releases.author: ElmoNeedsArson
releases.description:
Addressed Review Comments:

"fs": "^0.0.1-security"
That's not a dependency you can list here, it's automatically included if you are using Node.
Please remove it.

id: "3DModel"
This id and the command name are not really describing what the command is doing.
releases.name: Addressed Review Comments:
